meaning: vt.鎴樿儨,鍏嬫湇,鑳滆繃,寰佹湇vi.寰楄儨
examples: They had to overcome formidable obstacles.
He forced himself to subdue and overcome his fears.
Something gave me the strength to overcome the difficulty.
Those margins helped overcome Democratic dominance of urban areas and tighter contests in suburbs.
The Ng family has also overcome allegations that some of the fish they pack was caught illegally.
